name: Dependency Injection Pattern
source: Clean Code by Robert C. Martin, Chapter 11: Systems
problem: Classes create their own dependencies internally, coupling them to specific implementations and making them hard to test, replace, or reconfigure without modifying source code.
steps: Identify every dependency a class creates internally with 'new'.
Declare the dependency as a constructor parameter (preferred) or setter parameter.
Accept the dependency as an interface type, not a concrete class.
Remove the internal instantiation; the caller or a DI container supplies the instance.
Test by injecting a mock or stub; deploy by injecting the real implementation.
